bathroom-renovation.label: Bathroom Renovation
bathroom-renovation.description: Full bathroom renovation including tiling, plumbing, fixtures, and waterproofing.
bathroom-renovation.includes: Labour, fixtures (toilet, vanity, shower/bath), tiling, waterproofing, and plumbing connections.
bathroom-renovation.excludes: Building consent for structural changes, asbestos removal, electrical work beyond lighting.
tips: Get a licensed plumber for all drainage and water supply work
Budget 10–15% extra for unexpected issues behind walls
Choose fittings before quoting so labour scope is clear
bathroom-renovation.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

kitchen-renovation.label: Kitchen Renovation
kitchen-renovation.description: Kitchen renovation including cabinetry, benchtop, splashback, and connections.
kitchen-renovation.includes: Labour, cabinetry, benchtop, splashback, plumbing connections, basic electrical.
kitchen-renovation.excludes: Appliances (unless specified), structural changes, consent fees.
tips: Get separate quotes for cabinetry, plumbing, and electrical
Allow 6–12 weeks lead time for custom cabinetry
Use a project manager for full kitchen gut-and-redo
kitchen-renovation.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

decking.label: Deck Building
decking.description: New deck construction including framing, decking boards, and balustrades.
decking.includes: Consent (if needed), framing, posts, decking boards, fixings, and basic balustrades.
decking.excludes: Painting or staining (optional extra), pergola or shade structure.
tips: Decks over 1.5m above ground require building consent
Hardwood and composite last longer but cost more upfront
Get a fixed-price quote to avoid cost blowout
decking.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

painting.label: Interior & Exterior Painting
painting.description: Professional interior or exterior painting including preparation and two coats.
painting.includes: Surface preparation, filling, sanding, primer where needed, two coats of finish paint.
painting.excludes: Wallpaper removal (extra charge), plaster repair beyond minor filling.
tips: Good preparation is 80% of a quality paint job
Ask for a specification listing paint brand and sheen
Exterior repaints every 8–10 years extend cladding life significantly
painting.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

medium.label: Full home interior or exterior repaint

roofing.label: Roof Replacement
roofing.description: Full roof replacement including removal of old roofing, underlay, and new Colorsteel or tile.
roofing.includes: Removal of old roof, underlay, all flashings, new roofing material and fixings.
roofing.excludes: Guttering (quoted separately), fascia/soffit repairs, roof painting.
tips: Get quotes from licensed roofing contractors only
Colorsteel is the most cost-effective option in NZ
Consider insulation upgrade while the roof is off
roofing.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

fencing-and-gates.label: Fencing & Gates
fencing-and-gates.description: New fence installation including posts, rails, and palings or panels.
fencing-and-gates.includes: Post holes, concrete, posts, rails, palings or panels, and fixings.
fencing-and-gates.excludes: Removal of old fencing (usually extra cost), gates unless specified.
tips: Check boundary lines with your council before starting
Timber palings are cheapest but need treatment or painting
Get neighbour agreement in writing for boundary fences
fencing-and-gates.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

flooring.label: Flooring Installation
flooring.description: Flooring installation including timber, laminate, vinyl, tile, or carpet.
flooring.includes: Labour, underlay where needed, adhesives, and trims. Materials priced separately unless specified.
flooring.excludes: Subfloor repairs, furniture removal, tile removal (extra cost).
tips: Vinyl plank is the best value for wet areas like bathrooms and laundries
Engineered timber is more stable than solid timber in NZ's climate
Get subfloor checked for moisture before installing timber
flooring.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

insulation.label: Insulation Installation
insulation.description: Ceiling, underfloor, or wall insulation installation including batts or blown-in insulation.
insulation.includes: Labour and insulation material. Ceiling, underfloor, or wall options.
insulation.excludes: Removal of old wet/contaminated insulation (extra cost), very restricted access.
tips: Ceiling insulation gives the best return on investment
Check if you qualify for Warmer Kiwi Homes government subsidy
R3.6 is minimum recommended for NZ ceiling insulation
insulation.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

driveway.label: Driveway Construction
driveway.description: New driveway or resurfacing in concrete, asphalt, or pavers.
driveway.includes: Labour, base preparation, compaction, concrete/asphalt/pavers, and edging.
driveway.excludes: Removal of old driveway (usually extra), drainage work, consent if crossing footpath.
tips: Concrete outlasts asphalt by 15–20 years in NZ conditions
Exposed aggregate or brushed concrete adds grip and looks better
Get at least 2 quotes — driveway pricing varies significantly
driveway.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

landscaping-and-outdoors.label: Landscaping
landscaping-and-outdoors.description: Garden landscaping including lawn, planting, paths, and garden beds.
landscaping-and-outdoors.includes: Labour, topsoil, plants, lawn, garden paths, and irrigation if required.
landscaping-and-outdoors.excludes: Tree removal, major earthworks and consent for large structures.
tips: Hire a landscape designer for complex projects — saves cost in the long run
Native plants require less maintenance and suit NZ conditions
Ask if topsoil and plants are included in the quote
landscaping-and-outdoors.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

retaining-walls.label: Retaining Walls
retaining-walls.description: Retaining wall construction in timber, concrete block, or engineered stone.
retaining-walls.includes: Labour, materials (timber/block/stone), drainage behind wall, and concrete footings.
retaining-walls.excludes: Building consent for walls over 1.5m high, earthworks and soil disposal.
tips: Walls over 1.5m require building consent in most NZ councils
Always include drainage behind the wall to prevent failure
Concrete block outlasts timber but costs more upfront
retaining-walls.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

small.label: Short timber wall (≤6m, up to 1m high)

plasterboard.label: Plasterboard & Stopping
plasterboard.description: Plasterboard installation, stopping, and finishing ready for paint.
plasterboard.includes: Plasterboard supply and installation, all stopping coats, sanding to Level 4 finish.
tips: Level 4 stopping is standard for painted surfaces in NZ
Ensure stopping is fully dry before painting — rush jobs cause cracking
GIB Aqualine or equivalent is required in wet areas
plasterboard.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

guttering-and-drainage.label: Guttering & Drainage
guttering-and-drainage.description: Gutter replacement or new installation and stormwater drainage.
guttering-and-drainage.includes: Gutters, downpipes, brackets, sealants, and connection to stormwater drainage.
guttering-and-drainage.excludes: Roof repairs, stormwater drainage beyond the property boundary.
tips: Replace gutters every 20–30 years or when they start sagging or leaking
Aluminium gutters are the standard in NZ — avoid galvanised in coastal areas
Check downpipes run to proper stormwater drain, not just the garden
guttering-and-drainage.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

heating-ventilation-systems.label: Heating & Ventilation
heating-ventilation-systems.description: Heat pump, wood burner, or HRV ventilation system supply and installation.
heating-ventilation-systems.includes: Supply and installation of heat pump or HRV, electrical connection, and commissioning.
heating-ventilation-systems.excludes: Flue for wood burners (specialist), consent fees, electrical switchboard upgrade.
tips: Heat pumps are the most cost-effective heating in NZ — 3:1 efficiency ratio
Check your council rules on wood burner installation (many urban areas restrict)
HRV or DVS systems are worth it in damp NZ homes
heating-ventilation-systems.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

lighting.label: Lighting Installation
lighting.description: Interior or exterior lighting installation including downlights, feature lighting, and sensors.
lighting.includes: Labour, wiring, switch installation, and light fittings (if supply-and-install).
lighting.excludes: Fittings if customer-supplied, switchboard upgrades (quoted separately).
tips: LED downlights save significant power over halogen
Ensure your electrician is registered with the Electrical Workers Registration Board
Dimmer switches add cost but improve comfort and save power
lighting.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

large.label: New build or major refit (40+ lights)

asbestos-removal.label: Asbestos Removal
asbestos-removal.description: Licensed asbestos identification, removal, and disposal including clearance certificate.
asbestos-removal.includes: Site assessment, licensed removal, disposal, and written clearance certificate.
asbestos-removal.excludes: Asbestos testing if not yet done, reinstatement/repair work after removal.
tips: Never remove asbestos yourself — licensed removalist required by NZ law
Homes built before 1990 often contain asbestos in walls, ceilings, and floor tiles
Get a clearance certificate after removal — required for council CCC
asbestos-removal.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

medium.label: Medium area — wall linings or soffit (≤30m²)

glass-replacement.label: Glass & Glazing
glass-replacement.description: Window or door glass replacement including single, double glaze, and retrofit.
glass-replacement.includes: Glass supply and installation, sealing, and glazing beads. Frames if required.
glass-replacement.excludes: Full window frame replacement (separate quote), building consent for major changes.
tips: Double glazing reduces heat loss by ~50% vs single glaze
Retrofit double glazing is cheaper than full window replacement
Safety glass is required by NZ code near doors and low windows
glass-replacement.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

large.label: Full home re-glaze to double glaze

cladding.label: External Cladding
cladding.description: External wall cladding replacement or installation including weatherboards, Linea, or Hardie.
cladding.includes: Removal of old cladding if replacing, building wrap, new cladding, and flashings.
cladding.excludes: Painting (usually quoted separately), structural repairs to framing.
tips: Leaky home era (1994–2004) homes must be fully recladded if using direct-fix
Cavity-drain cladding systems are now standard — avoid direct-fix
James Hardie and weathertex are popular mid-range cladding options in NZ
cladding.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025

structures.label: Garages & Sleepouts
structures.description: Garage, carport, sleepout, or garden shed construction to lockup stage.
structures.includes: Foundation/slab, framing, roofing, cladding, windows, and door to lockup stage.
structures.excludes: Building consent, internal lining/fitout, electrical, plumbing.
tips: Most garages and sleepouts require building consent — allow 4–8 weeks
Prefab kitset garages are cheaper but require consent if attached to house
Insulate if used as a sleepout — NZ building code requires it
structures.source: NZ market research (Builderscrack, Rawlinsons, industry data) 2025
